taxonomy: Trichera sylvatica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Trichera, which is a small genus of plants native to tropical Africa. The family Apocynaceae is a large and diverse group of plants, commonly known as dogbane or oleander family. The classification of Trichera sylvatica is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular analysis. Further research is needed to confirm the accuracy of its classification.
uses: Trichera sylvatica is a plant species that has been traditionally used in some cultures for its potential medicinal properties. Its roots are said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing pain and inflammation.
